title: Buffer pool manager with LRU-K replacement
description: Implement the buffer pool manager: fixed-size array of 8KB page frames, page_table HashMap<PageId,FrameId>, LRU-K replacer (K=2). Implement pin/unpin protocol (pinned pages cannot be evicted), dirty page tracking with AtomicBool, WAL protocol enforcement (page_lsn ≤ flushed_wal_lsn before dirty page write). LRU-K tracks last K access timestamps per frame, evicts frame with oldest K-th-last access for sequential scan resistance. Implement configurable pool size (default 256MB = 32768 frames), page pre-fetching hints for sequential scan read-ahead (32 pages), and page checksum validation on read. Include unit tests: pin/unpin counting, eviction of unpinned pages, pinned pages surviving eviction, dirty writeback, LRU-K ordering preference, sequential scan resistance, and all-pages-pinned error handling.
result_summary:
Done. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`sqld/src/storage/buffer_pool.rs`** — Two fixes to `evict_frame`:
1. Added WAL protocol enforcement: dirty pages can only be written during eviction when `page_lsn ≤ flushed_wal_lsn` (line 547-554)
2. On WAL violation, the frame is restored to the replacer so it isn't permanently lost (line 549-550)

**`sqld/tests/test_buffer_pool.rs`** — Added `wal_protocol_enforcement_on_eviction` test (lines 328-358) that verifies:
- Evicting a dirty page with `page_lsn > flushed_wal_lsn` fails
- After advancing the WAL LSN, the eviction succeeds
- The frame isn't permanently lost after a WAL violation

The existing test file already had comprehensive coverage for all 7 required test categories:

| Test | Coverage |
|------|----------|
| Pin/unpin counting | `pin_unpin_counting` |
| Eviction of unpinned pages | `eviction_of_unpinned_pages` |
| Pinned pages surviving eviction | `pinned_pages_survive_eviction` |
| Dirty writeback | `dirty_page_writeback`, `dirty_eviction_writes_to_disk` |
| LRU-K ordering preference | `lru_k_ordering_preference` |
| Sequential scan resistance | `sequential_scan_resistance` |
| All-pages-pinned error | `all_pages_pinned_error` |

Plus additional tests for WAL protocol, checksum validation, prefetch, flush_all, delete_page, new_page, and configurable pool size.
